pitch: Open-source infrastructure for private messaging and self-hosted meetings — chatmail relays, video, and tools around the Delta Chat ecosystem.
projects_lede: Communication tools you can host yourself — from chatmail relays to video meetings and the docs around them.
group_meet_desc: Self-hosted video that you run yourself.
group_sdk_desc: Build clients on the chatmail protocol.
group_learn_desc: Docs for the Delta Chat ecosystem.
project_bedrud_blurb: Self-hosted video meeting platform. One binary, unlimited users, no per-seat tax.
project_madmail_blurb: Rust chatmail relay for Delta Chat — encrypted, federated, one binary. Batteries included.
project_madexchanger_blurb: HTTPS relay for routing messages between Madmail servers.
project_madexchanger_php_blurb: Pull-based HTTP email relay for Madmail (PHP implementation).
project_madcore_blurb: High-level TypeScript library for Delta Chat–compatible web messengers (JSON-RPC over WebSocket).
project_madmail_admin_blurb: Web dashboard for managing Madmail instances.
project_relay_ping_blurb: Chatmail relay interoperability tooling — latency matrix and WebXDC viewer.
project_deltachat_wiki_blurb: Wiki and guides for Delta Chat, chatmail, WebXDC, and related tooling.
principles_lede: We build communication infrastructure for people who care about privacy, ownership, and running software they understand.
principle_selfhosted_body: Your messages and meetings run on hardware you control — not rented seats on someone else’s cloud.
principle_encrypted_body: PGP-first mail policy, federated chatmail, and privacy-minded defaults across the stack.
principle_lowops_body: Single binaries, simple install paths, and tools built so small teams can operate them without a platform squad.
principle_opensource_body: Code you can read, fork, and audit. Licenses vary by project (AGPL, Apache, GPL) — always free to study.
footer_note: © {year} TheMadOrg · project licenses vary (AGPL / Apache / GPL)
